title: Figma vs Sketch
description: Compare Figma and Sketch across collaboration, cross-platform support, prototyping, and design system management to find the right design tool for your team.
verdict: Figma's browser-based platform enables real-time collaboration across Windows, Mac, and Linux without file sending or version conflicts, with auto-layout and component variant systems that keep design systems consistent across teams of 50+ designers. Sketch offers a polished native Mac experience with excellent offline performance, a mature plugin ecosystem of 900+ extensions, and support for open file formats that avoids vendor lock-in. Cross-platform and remote teams will prefer Figma for its universal accessibility and community-driven plugin library. Mac-only design teams that work offline frequently and value a focused native tool will still find Sketch a capable choice for vector editing and design system management.

faqs.question: Is Figma better than Sketch?
faqs.answer: For most teams in 2026, yes. Figma offers real-time collaboration that lets multiple designers work on the same file simultaneously, runs on Windows, Mac, and Linux via the browser, and has a larger community plugin ecosystem. Sketch remains a strong choice for solo Mac-based designers who need full offline capability and prefer native macOS performance over browser-based tools.

faqs.question: Can I use Figma files in Sketch?
faqs.answer: Not directly. Figma and Sketch use different file formats. You can export designs to common formats like SVG or PNG, but full file conversion between the two tools is limited and often loses fidelity.

faqs.question: Does Figma have offline mode?
faqs.answer: Figma has a limited offline mode through its desktop app, but it is primarily an online tool. Sketch offers full offline editing since it is a native Mac application.

faqs.question: Which tool is better for UI design?
faqs.answer: Both are excellent for UI design. Figma's component variants, auto-layout, and prototyping tools make it slightly more versatile for responsive designs. Sketch's symbols system is mature but less flexible than Figma's modern approach.
